findings.evidence: `FiniteProduct.lean:88-100` defines `prodInl`, `prodInr`, and `prodDesc` before `end Semiring`, while `FiniteProduct.lean:248-282` proves `prodInl_desc`/`prodInr_desc` only under `[CommRing R]`; the ambient lemmas are semiring-level at `Product.lean:519` and `Product.lean:528`.
findings.fix: Move `prodInl_desc` and `prodInr_desc` to the semiring section alongside `prodDesc`, using the existing semiring-level ambient lemmas.
findings.issue: `prodInl`, `prodInr`, and `prodDesc` are public under `CommSemiring`, but their basic characteristic lemmas `prodInl_desc` and `prodInr_desc` are only available after entering the `CommRing` section.

summary: The finite product wrapper still exposes semiring-level inclusion/desc constructors without the matching semiring-level composition API, so users must unfold through the wrapper for basic use.
